mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals stronger real economic activity, which increases corporate earnings visibility and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in >60% of periods when INDPRO rises by >0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 3.5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in >50% of instances where UNRATE declines below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPIAUCSL (inflation) and FEDFUNDS (interest rates) signal central bank responsiveness to inflation, which stabilizes real rates and supports equity valuations over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise by >0.3% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) reduce liquidity in fixed-income markets, forcing capital into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during high-volatility regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in >50% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in >60% of periods when INDPRO declines by >0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 200k signals robust labor market growth that increases consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in >50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate improving demand for residential construction, which boosts related sectors and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in >60% of periods when HOUST rises by >10% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in RSAFS (consumer spending) and GDP (GDPC1) signal weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate revenues and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive when both RSAFS and GDPC1 decline by >0.3% quarter-over-quarter.
